ab: i Akav a nia umaq ta kakedriyan anan aken, ta sema gakku anga ken, patjavat amen a sema Tauyun, saka izuazua angamen i lizuk a kikavaljut a patjetucu.
ch: 小時候我們家住在屏東，上學以後，就搬到了桃園，一直在都市裡生活。
en: When we were young, our family lived in Donghe. After I started school, we moved to Taoyuan and have been living in the city ever since.

ab: makalap anga aken tua taigakku, sa ku kitulu tuazuz masanitalj a patjara nu kacalisian a kakudakudan a tulu, avanazua kemeljang aken tua napenakaizuazua a tja kakudan a kacalisian i Taiwan.
ch: 上了大學以後，我修了一堂關於原住民文化的課程，瞭解到臺灣原住民族有各式各樣豐富的文化。
en: After I went to college, I took a course on aboriginal culture and learned that the aboriginal people in Taiwan have various and rich cultures.

ab: manasika, tucu a cavilj a pulavay tua zulezulan, vaik aken katua ku kaka a tjavulung a uqaljay a sema qinaljan a kivadaq tua ramaljemaljeng, pasa kikeljang amen tua nitjen a kakudakudan.
ch: 因此，今年暑假時，我和哥哥回到部落訪問老人家，想要瞭解自己族群的文化。
en: So this summer, my brother and I went back to our tribe to visit the elderly, hoping to understand the culture of our own ethnic group.

ab: aluangapuluq a cavilj ni vuvu, ljakua nu tja pacunan nasa’edriyan anan sakamaya.
ch: 老人家已經八十歲了，但是看起來還像中年人。
en: The old man is already eighty years old, but he still looks like a middle-aged man.

ab: ku kivadaqan timadju tu anemanema kinacaqu a kai, tauliq aravac a qivu tua penayuanan, macaqu a dremipungan, a dremekaiyan katua penairangan uta.
ch: 我問他會哪些語言，他的排灣語說得很流利，也會說日本話、魯凱語和閩南語。
en: I asked him which languages he knew, and he was fluent in Saisiyat, as well as Atayal, Hakka and Japanese.

ab: ta makakivadaq anga ken tjaimadju, djaljepan ta kakedrian anan timadju, na kitulu tua dremipungan, azua cekelj se drekai zuku, saka pizua tua sengsengan nimadju nasekeljang tua tjuruvu a pairang, manasika liav a kinacaqu a kai nimadju.
ch: 我問他才知道，原來他小時候就學日本話，太太是鲁凱族人，而他工作上認識許多漢人，所以他會說這麼多語言。
en: I asked him to find out that he learned Japanese when he was a child, his wife is an Atayal, and he knows many Hakkas at work, so he can speak so many languages.

ab: itua nia kinaqepuan, liav aravac a sitjaucikecikel tua patjara semicuacuayan a caquan nua qemaljup.
ch: 我們訪談的時候，老人家談論了許多關於狩獵的傳統知識。
en: During our interview, the old man talked about a lot of traditional knowledge about hunting.

ab: ljai inika men a sepualalang, ljakua nia linuing a paka tua dingwa, maqati a nia umaljen anan a kiladenga.
ch: 雖然我們沒有辦法完全聽懂，但是我們有用手機錄影，可以再仔細聆聽。
en: Although we could't fully understand it, we could record it on our cell phones and listen to it more.

ab: ta kakedrian anan aken, inika ken a nakemeljang tua tja kakudan a paiwan, nasemiaq tu kacalisian aken pai.
ch: 小時候我不瞭解自己族群的文化，對於自己是原住民有點不好意思。
en: When I was young, I didn't understand the culture of my ethnic group, and I was a little embarrassed that I was an aborigine.

ab: nu tjaliav a ku kinljangan, tja ljemaui tu Paiwan zuku aken, napakisumalji, sedjaljep tu ku sikipasukuda uta.
ch: 瞭解愈多以後，愈覺得自己身為排灣族人，是很獨特的，也很值得驕傲。
en: The more I know, the more I feel that being a Saisiyat is unique and something to be proud of.
